title: Investeringsforeningen Sparinvest suspenderer handel af udvalgte afdelinger
description: Under henvisning til Nasdaq Copenhagens regler for udstedere af investeringsbeviser skal ID-Sparinvest, Filial af Sparinvest S.A., Luxembourg hermed på vegne af de berørte afdelinger i Investeringsforeningen Sparinvest offentliggøre, at indberetning af indre værdi til Nasdaq Copenhagen er udeblevet grundet tekniske udfordringer. Handel med de berørte afdelinger ønskes derfor midlertidigt suspenderet.

title: Anderson confirmed as NASA deputy administrator
description: WASHINGTON — The U.S. Senate confirmed Matt Anderson on May 18 as NASA’s deputy administrator, the second-in-command of the space agency.

title: ESA-China SMILE mission lifts off to deliver first global images of Earth’s magnetosphere
description: KOUROU, French Guiana — The SMILE mission developed jointly by the European Space Agency and China has reached orbit after more than a decade of preparations and cooperation.

title: European imaging companies step in to fill warzone gap
description: MILAN – As U.S. satellite imagery companies have pulled back from sharing visuals of Iran and the broader area around the Gulf conflict, European Earth-observation firms are moving to fill the vacuum.

title: Four NASA payloads to fly on Astrolab’s first lunar rover
description: WASHINGTON — Astrolab’s first lunar rover will carry four NASA payloads on a mission planned to launch later this year.

title: Inside Golden Dome’s push to court commercial tech firms and investors
description: WASHINGTON — Pentagon officials overseeing Golden Dome are trying to convince commercial space founders, venture investors and software companies that missile defense can be built more like a modern technology platform than a traditional weapons system.

title: Tomorrow.io adds $35 million to DeepSky funding round
description: TAMPA, Fla. — Weather intelligence provider Tomorrow.io has added $35 million to its latest funding round, bringing the total to $210 million to accelerate development of a next-generation constellation for gathering atmospheric data.

title: House bill restores funding for TraCSS
description: WASHINGTON — A House appropriations bill would reverse plans by the administration to stop development of a civil space traffic management system.

title: New CSF Report Sees Up To 7,000+ Satellites Launched Annually By Mid 2030’s, Highlights The Challenges With US Launch Infrastructure
The next war will be won — or lost — in orbit
description: We in the West have learned many things from the conflict in Ukraine. Four years on from its full-scale invasion — the war in fact having started in 2014 — we find ourselves, or should find ourselves, with a far better grasp of the nature of war, and a far greater sense of the dangers we face. That is reflected most visibly in promises across Western Europe to increase defense spending. But what is the concrete impact of this? As many commentators have said, it’s not just what you spend, but

title: Zenk Space raises $26 million, targets June debut launch
description: KOUROU, French Guiana — China’s Zenk Space has secured 180 million yuan ahead of the planned June debut of its Zhihang-1 kerolox rocket, the company’s first orbital launch attempt.

title: NASA releases final RFP for Mars communications orbiter
description: WASHINGTON — NASA has released the final request for proposals for a Mars telecommunications system, confirming requirements that limit the companies that can bid on it.

title: York Space defends growth strategy as SDA programs are reshaped
description: WASHINGTON — Just months after its public debut, York Space Systems is confronting a question that goes to the core of its business model: what happens when the Pentagon program that fueled its rise is fundamentally reorganized.

title: Space Force awards Northrop Grumman $398 million satellite contract
description: WASHINGTON — The U.S. Space Force awarded Northrop Grumman a $398 million contract to build a prototype communications satellite intended to demonstrate anti-jamming technologies for military operations in contested environments, the Space Systems Command said May 15.

title: SpaceX launches CRS-34 cargo mission to ISS
description: Updated May 16 to include CLARREO Pathfinder.

title: Starlink Mobile challengers back proposed telco-led D2D joint venture
description: TAMPA, Fla. — A planned direct-to-device (D2D) joint venture between AT&T, T-Mobile and Verizon has divided operators behind the satellites needed to connect ordinary smartphones from space.

title: Joint ESA-China SMILE mission set for launch to study Earth’s magnetic shield
description: HELSINKI — A pioneering joint ESA-China science mission to study Earth’s magnetosphere is set to launch May 19, after a decade of preparations.

title: Virgin Galactic reaffirms plans to begin commercial service this year
description: WASHINGTON — Virgin Galactic said May 14 it remains on track, technically and financially, to start commercial flights of its next-generation suborbital spaceplane before the end of the year.

title: Chinese satellite maker MinoSpace seeks $736 million in IPO
description: HELSINKI — Chinese satellite maker MinoSpace has seen its initial public offering application accepted, seeking $736 million for constellation and product expansion plans.

title: Cowboy files plans for up to 20,000 orbital data centers
description: TAMPA, Fla. — Cowboy Space has filed plans with the Federal Communications Commission for a 20,000-satellite “Stampede” orbital data center constellation, shortly after raising $275 million to develop rockets whose upper stages would serve as the computing platforms.

title: Intuitive Machines to buy ground station company
description: WASHINGTON — Intuitive Machines has agreed to purchase a company that operates ground stations in the United States and United Kingdom to help build out a lunar communications network.

title: Golden Dome chief pushes back on $1.2 trillion CBO estimate
description: WASHINGTON — Gen. Michael Guetlein on May 14 forcefully rejected a new Congressional Budget Office estimate that President Donald Trump’s proposed Golden Dome missile defense initiative could cost $1.2 trillion over 20 years, arguing the analysis relies on outdated assumptions and does not reflect the architecture the Pentagon is actually pursuing.

title: Iridium to take over Aireon to expand aviation safety business
description: TAMPA, Fla. — Iridium Communications is buying the rest of the Aireon aircraft-tracking venture its connectivity constellation hosts to push further into aviation safety, surveillance and data services.

title: CEO Series: Chris Quilty looks at the future of the space industry
AST SpaceMobile may use ULA’s Vulcan
description: WASHINGTON — AST SpaceMobile may launch some of its direct-to-device satellites on United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket to expand the launch options for its constellation.

title: Germany to deploy Patriot battery to Turkey, relieving US forces on NATO’s southeastern flank
description: Germany’s contribution to intercepting Iranian missiles is framed in Berlin as an exercise in burden-sharing.

title: Ukraine declares its first homegrown guided aerial bomb combat-ready
description: A 250-kilogram answer to Russia's daily glide-bomb campaign and Kyiv's dependence on Western precision strike capabilities for mid-range targets.

title: US Air Force looks to convert offshore oil rigs into rocket recovery platforms
description: An Air Force plan calls for old oil platforms to become Sea-based Recovery Stations for the U.S. Space Force and private spaceflight companies.

title: Former University of Michigan researcher accused of hiding Chinese military drone ties
description: Investigators say Chuan Wang claimed modest roles on visa applications when in fact he was publicly known in China for his military drones.

title: US Army’s 7th Infantry Division, 1st MDTF to merge as Multi-Domain Command-Pacific
description: The two-star Multi-Domain Command-Pacific will merge the 7th ID’s Stryker infantry brigades and a combat aviation brigade with a multidomain task force.

title: US Army to receive thousands of Barracuda-500M cruise missiles in Anduril deal
description: The Pentagon’s Low-Cost Containerized Missiles program aims to obtain over 10,000 low-cost cruise missiles over a three-year span.

title: Army leaders in hot seat over Poland deployment cancellation
description: Lawmakers questioned the timing and the reasons, lambasting the order that Rep. Don Bacon, R-Neb., said sent a “terrible message to Russia and our allies.”

title: No sound of silence: US soldiers train eyes — and ears — for drone swarms
description: The U.S. Army is moving beyond battling individual drone threats as it experiments with tactics to combat throngs of unmanned aircraft in saturated skies.

title: USS Gerald R. Ford to return from 11-month deployment on Saturday
description: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is finally returning home after etching its name into Navy history books.

title: Canada-led brigade in Latvia moves beyond tripwire role, commander says
description: “Right now I have a brigade, there is nothing on the other side of the border that can take out this brigade,” the unit commander told Defense News.

title: US Marines practice seizing remote islands in Philippine exercise
description: Based on Oahu, MADIS crews face limitations when live-firing at home, so Konien was enthusiastic about the opportunity to fire rounds and missiles abroad.

title: DoD faces mounting pressure to pass clean audit for the first time
description: To date, the DoD has never passed a full, clean audit since they first became mandated in 2018.

title: Near Russian border, NATO grapples with ground robots in combat
description: “We are a little bit behind because we’ve been using only the air drones,” a Latvian commander said.

title: China fires verbal warning shot at US over Taiwan
description: Any misstep over Taiwan could push the U.S. and China toward direct confrontation, Chinese leader Xi Jinping told President Donald Trump.

title: Lawmakers push for domestic shipbuilding fixes as US Navy explores overseas options
description: The service released its fiscal 2027 shipbuilding plan on Monday, outlining the possibility of the U.S. manufacturing ships at foreign shipyards.

title: Iran military threat is diminished but not eliminated, CENTCOM chief says
description: Adm. Brad Cooper said the U.S. had severely degraded Iran’s warfighting capacity, including the elimination of roughly 90% of its inventory of naval mines.

title: Blacklists, corruption and frontline needs: Ukraine tackles an arms-export puzzle
description: After years of struggling to arm its one million active-duty soldiers, Kyiv has been wary of allowing its domestic producers to sell their weapons abroad.

title: Israel to extend F-35I range amid war with Iran

title: Japan fires first-ever missiles from Philippine soil

title: No Indo-Pacific peace without industry surge and burden sharing, US general says
description: Pushing industry to far-reaching frontlines — and having allies share the load — is vital to Indo-Pacific security, U.S. Army Gen. Xavier Brunson stressed.

title: US Navy could run out of money by July, top officer warns
description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told lawmakers at a budget hearing Tuesday that the Navy might have to modify training and operations.

title: Contractor awarded $3.5 billion to build out US Coast Guard’s Arctic Security Cutters fleet
description: The five cutters to be delivered by Davie Defense will represent a new class of Arctic icebreakers.
